output: Completed batch 2 tasks T2, T6, T7, T8, and T10. Threaded `config_version` through the editor save path with RPC-based optimistic locking and conflict-dialog surfacing, added the `ai-timeline-agent` scaffold plus Groq tool schemas, implemented the pure timeline/session tool helpers, added the client session hooks with direct realtime subscription and auto-continuation, verified the touched files with targeted ESLint plus `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false`, and wrote the batch checkpoint.

task_updates.executor_notes: Threaded `configVersion` end-to-end through the editor load/save path: `DataProvider.loadTimeline()` now returns `{ config, configVersion }`, `SupabaseDataProvider.saveTimeline()` calls the `update_timeline_config_versioned` RPC and throws `TimelineVersionConflictError` on an empty result, `TimelineData` carries the loaded version, and `useTimelineSave()` keeps a version ref synchronized across poll/reload/save success. On conflict, autosave now stops retrying, sets `saveStatus` to `error`, and the existing remote-conflict dialog opens through `useTimelineRealtime`'s new error-state effect instead of silently overwriting local edits. Verification: targeted ESLint passed, `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` exited 0, and `git diff --check` passed for the touched files.

Added the `ai-timeline-agent` scaffold with `withEdgeRequest(...)` and `NO_SESSION_RUNTIME_OPTIONS`, strict POST body parsing for `{ session_id, user_message? }`, JWT auth, session ownership checks, cancelled-session rejection, user-turn persistence, and processing-state writes. Added `tools.ts` with the Groq function-calling schemas for all timeline/session tools so T9 can plug the live loop into a stable tool surface. Verification: targeted ESLint passed, `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` exited 0, and the response paths for unauthenticated/forbidden/cancelled sessions are explicit in the scaffold. The iterative LLM tool loop itself remains the planned T9 follow-up on top of this scaffold.

Implemented the pure timeline manipulation helpers in `timeline-tools.ts`. The mutating tools (`move_clip`, `trim_clip`, `set_clip_property`, `delete_clip`, `add_text_clip`) clone the incoming config before editing, `view_timeline` produces a read-only enriched timeline summary, and `find_issues` detects gaps, overlaps, and asset-duration overruns with an explicit comment documenting that transcript-based mid-sentence-cut detection is unavailable in the current provider. Verification: targeted ESLint passed, `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` exited 0, and the tool implementations all return the required `{ result, config? }` shape.

Implemented `message_user`, `done`, and `create_generation_task` in `session-tools.ts`. `create_generation_task` calls the existing `/functions/v1/create-task` edge function via HTTP using the service-role key, includes `task_type`, `params`, `project_id`, and `idempotency_key`, and restricts v1 support to `image_generation`. To avoid making retry deduplication worse, the idempotency key falls back to a deterministic SHA-256 hash of the payload when the caller does not supply one. Verification: targeted ESLint passed, `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` exited 0, and the fetch body matches the existing `createTask` contract fields.

Added `useAgentSessions`, `useAgentSession`, `useCreateSession`, `useSendMessage`, and `useCancelSession` in `useAgentSession.ts`. The single-session hook uses a direct `supabase.channel()` subscription to `postgres_changes` on `timeline_agent_sessions` filtered by `id=eq.<sessionId>`, invalidates queries on updates, and removes the channel on cleanup. `useSendMessage` invokes `ai-timeline-agent`, auto-reinvokes after 300ms on `status === 'continue'`, and stops after 10 consecutive continuations with a surfaced notice. Verification: targeted ESLint passed, `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` exited 0, `git diff --check` passed, and the direct realtime plumbing avoids the shared `RealtimeConnection` system per the plan flag.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: `loadTimeline()` now returns `configVersion`, `saveTimeline()` uses the versioned RPC, and the save hook treats an empty RPC result as a `TimelineVersionConflictError` that stops autosave retrying and opens the existing conflict dialog via `saveStatus='error'`. I did not run a live two-tab concurrent-edit test in this batch.

By inspection, unauthenticated requests hit `withEdgeRequest(...)` auth handling and return 401, `session.user_id !== auth.userId` returns 403, and cancelled sessions return 409 from the new scaffold before any further processing. I did not execute live HTTP requests against the edge function in this batch.

Each timeline tool returns `{ result, config? }`, the mutating tools deep-clone with `structuredClone(config)` before editing, and `find_issues()` sorts clips per track so it can report track-local gaps and overlaps plus asset-duration overruns. Mid-sentence cuts are explicitly left out with a code comment because transcript data is not available here.

`create_generation_task()` calls `${SUPABASE_URL}/functions/v1/create-task` via `fetch`, not a direct table insert, and its request body includes `task_type`, `params`, `project_id`, `dependant_on: null`, and `idempotency_key`. The implementation currently restricts supported task types to `image_generation` as planned.

The client hook now uses a direct `supabase.channel()` subscription on `timeline_agent_sessions` updates, invalidates the session queries on UPDATE, and removes the channel on cleanup. Auto-continuation is bounded to 10 consecutive `continue` responses with a surfaced notice. I did not run an end-to-end realtime session update during this batch.
